headline: Trump Administration Finalizes 2.48% Medicare Advantage Payment Hike For 2027, Sending Insurer Stocks Surging
summary: The Trump administration on Monday raised the amount it will pay private insurers to run Medicare health plans for seniors, setting rates well above a proposal that had unsettled markets earlier this year. The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services set the payment increase at a net average of 2.48%, adding more than $13 billion in payments to Medicare Advantage plans in 2027. That was far above the 0.09% increase the agency had floated in January, which had pushed insurer stocks lower. Don't M

headline: Couples may need more than $400,000 to cover health-care costs in retirement, even with Medicare
summary: A 65-year-old couple may need about $400,000 to have a 90% chance of covering health costs over the course of retirement.
